---
title: "Kreview Intelligence Dashboard"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme:
      - cyborg
      - custom.scss
    embed-resources: true
    html-math-method: katex
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        <style>
          .legend .scrollbox { clip-path: none !important; }
          g.legend rect.bg { fill-opacity: 0 !important; }
        </style>
jupyter: python3
execute:
  cache: false
  echo: false
  debug: true
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
#| echo: false
# Quarto injects -P params here; defaults are for local development only
matrix_path = "output/FSC_gene_matrix.parquet"
feature_name = "FSC_gene"
cvd_safe = False
shap_samples = 500
shap_features = 10
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
# ── Setup: imports, renderer config, data loading ──────────────────────────
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import json
import joblib
import warnings
from pathlib import Path
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import plotly.io as pio
from IPython.display import Markdown, HTML, display
import itables

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

pio.renderers._render_on_display = False
_plotly_loaded = False

import os
def show_plotly(fig, filename=None):
    global _plotly_loaded
    if isinstance(fig, go.Figure):
        if filename:
            try:
                os.makedirs("static_plots", exist_ok=True)
                fig.write_image(f"static_plots/{filename}.png", scale=2)
            except Exception:
                pass
        # Prevent clipping horizontally and vertically, including a heavy bottom padding for bottom-aligned vertical legend
        fig.update_layout(margin=dict(b=150, t=50, l=10, r=10))
        html_str = fig.to_html(
            full_html=False,
            include_plotlyjs='cdn' if not _plotly_loaded else False,
            include_mathjax=False,
            config={"responsive": True}
        )
        _plotly_loaded = True
        display(HTML(html_str))
    elif isinstance(fig, (str, Markdown)):
        display(fig)
    else:
        display(Markdown(f"> Unexpected output type: {type(fig)}"))

pio.templates.default = "plotly_dark"
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

from kreview.eval_engine import (
    LABEL_ORDER, LABEL_COLORS, plot_violin, plot_density,
    plot_feature_vs_vaf, plot_feature_importance, plot_roc_curves,
    set_theme
)

# Sanitize potential string injection from Quarto CLI
is_cvd_safe = str(cvd_safe).lower() == "true"

set_theme(is_cvd_safe)

if is_cvd_safe:
    p_models = ["#0072B2", "#CC79A7", "#F0E442"]
else:
    p_models = ["#ff9b42", "#E85D75", "#9C27B0"] # Orange, Hot Pink, Purple

# L-01: Extracted Theme Constants
THEME = {
    # Dynamically toggle between CVD-safe and neon
    "labels": LABEL_COLORS,
    "models": p_models,
    "model_map": {"Logistic Regression": p_models[0], "Random Forest": p_models[1], "XGBoost": p_models[2]},
    "bg": "#1a1d23",
    "grid": "#2e3440",
    "text": "#e0e6ed",
    "axis": "#88c0d0"
}

LAYOUT_DEFAULTS = dict(
    autosize=True,
    paper_bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)',
    font=dict(color='#e0e6ed'),
    title_x=0.5,
    legend=dict(
        orientation="v",
        yanchor="top",
        y=-0.15,
        xanchor="center",
        x=0.5,
        font=dict(size=11, color="#c9d1d9")
    )
)

df = pd.read_parquet(matrix_path)

meta_cols = {"SAMPLE_ID", "PATIENT_ID", "CANCER_TYPE", "CANCER_TYPE_DETAILED",
             "ONCOTREE_CODE", "SAMPLE_TYPE", "GENE_PANEL", "access_version",
             "has_impact_match", "n_impact_confirmed", "has_paired_impact",
             "has_snv", "n_somatic_snvs", "n_total_somatic_snvs", "max_vaf",
             "has_sv", "n_somatic_svs", "has_cna", "n_cna_events", "label",
             "min_vaf_used", "min_variants_used", "min_fragments_used",
             "sample_id", "filename"}
numeric_cols = [c for c in df.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns if c not in meta_cols]

eval_path = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_eval_stats.parquet"
if eval_path.exists():
    eval_df = pd.read_parquet(eval_path)
    if "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in eval_df.columns:
        best = eval_df.dropna(subset=["cohens_d_true_vs_healthy"]).nlargest(1, "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy")
        top_col = best["feature_column"].iloc[0] if len(best) > 0 else numeric_cols[0]
    else:
        top_col = numeric_cols[0]
else:
    eval_df = None
    top_col = numeric_cols[0] if numeric_cols else None

# ── Load model results ──
model_path = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_model_results.json"
_shap_display_n = int(shap_features)  # Controls VISUALIZATION only, not computation
if model_path.exists():
    with open(model_path) as f:
        mres = json.load(f)
    auc_rf = mres.get('auc_rf', 0)
    auc_lr = mres.get('auc_lr', 0)
    auc_xgb = mres.get('auc_xgb', 0)
    
    # Use ALL features the model was trained on — SHAP requires matching shape
    top_feats = mres.get("top_features", numeric_cols)
else:
    mres = None
    auc_rf = auc_lr = auc_xgb = 0
    top_feats = numeric_cols

# Load Models
import shap
lr_model = None
rf_model = None
xgb_model = None

shap_df_sample = None
X_shap = None
y_shap = None

# We cache predictions for ROC and Density
lr_preds = None
rf_preds = None
xgb_preds = None
rf_preds_all = None

if mres is not None and top_feats:
    rf_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_rf_model.joblib"
    lr_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_lr_model.joblib"
    xgb_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_xgb_model.joblib"
    
    if lr_joblib.exists(): lr_model = joblib.load(lr_joblib)
    if rf_joblib.exists(): rf_model = joblib.load(rf_joblib)
    if xgb_joblib.exists(): xgb_model = joblib.load(xgb_joblib)
        
    model_label = df["label"].isin([
        "True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+", 
        "Healthy Normal", "Possible ctDNA-", "Possible ctDNA−"
    ])

    # ── KDE density predictions (subsample full df to save memory) ──
    if rf_model is not None:
        try:
            _kde_n = min(2000, len(df))
            kde_idx = df.sample(n=_kde_n, random_state=99).index
            rf_preds_all = rf_model.predict_proba(df.loc[kde_idx, top_feats].fillna(0))[:, 1]
            kde_labels = df.loc[kde_idx, "label"].values
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[WARN] RF KDE prediction failed: {e}")
            rf_preds_all = None
            kde_labels = None
    
    # ── SHAP subsample ──
    shap_df = df[model_label]
    _max_shap = int(shap_samples)
    
    if len(shap_df) > 0:
        if len(shap_df) > _max_shap:
            sample_idx = shap_df.sample(n=_max_shap, random_state=42).index
        else:
            sample_idx = shap_df.index
        
        X_shap = shap_df.loc[sample_idx, top_feats].fillna(0)
        y_shap = shap_df.loc[sample_idx, "label"].isin(["True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+"]).astype(int).values
        shap_df_sample = shap_df.loc[sample_idx, ["label"] + (["sample_id"] if "sample_id" in shap_df.columns else [])].copy()

        # Calculate Predictions on SHAP subset only
        if lr_model is not None:
            try:
                lr_preds = lr_model.predict_proba(X_shap)[:, 1]
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[WARN] LR prediction failed: {e}")
        if rf_model is not None:
            try:
                rf_preds = rf_model.predict_proba(X_shap)[:, 1]
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[WARN] RF prediction failed: {e}")
        if xgb_model is not None:
            try:
                xgb_preds = xgb_model.predict_proba(X_shap)[:, 1]
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[WARN] XGBoost prediction failed: {e}")

    # ── Free the full dataframe — only keep what dashboards need ──
    del shap_df
    import gc; gc.collect()
```

# Global Glossary {.sidebar}

### ctDNA Biological Label Maps

To interpret the clinical matrices correctly, understand how the machine categorizes liquid biopsy fragmentomics via physical genomic verification patterns.

**Positive Class (ctDNA Detection)**:
- **`True ctDNA+`**: Sample has 100% verified mutant molecules mapping perfectly to the patient's MSK-IMPACT tissue profiles.
- **`Possible ctDNA+`**: Sub-threshold mutations mathematically consistent with circulating tumor traces.

**Negative Class (Healthy Baselines)**:
- **`Healthy Normal`**: Medically confirmed healthy volunteer buffy coat / plasma. Purely white-blood-cell derived circulating DNA. Peak structural size of 167bp.
- **`Possible ctDNA-`**: Advanced cancer patient where zero mathematical tumor molecules were detectable in plasma, acting as a biological negative pseudo-control.

---

*Because tumor cells undergo massive apoptotic and necrotic stress, their circulating DNA degrades fundamentally differently than healthy leukocytes—creating biologically distinct "fragmentomic signatures" across size distributions, end-motif sequences, and nucleosome positioning footprints mathematically detectable in plasma.*

# Machine Learning Diagnostics {orientation="rows" scrolling="true"}

## Row {height="15%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Random Forest AUC"
dict(value=f"{auc_rf:.3f}" if auc_rf else "N/A", color="success", icon="tree")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "XGBoost AUC"
dict(value=f"{auc_xgb:.3f}" if auc_xgb else "N/A", color="info", icon="lightning")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Logistic Regression"
dict(value=f"{auc_lr:.3f}" if auc_lr else "N/A", color="warning", icon="graph-up")
```

## Row {.tabset}

### Multi-Model Performance

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## 📐 Mathematical Interpretation: Evaluation Metrics (Click to Expand)
This grouped bar chart tests whether the single fragmentomic feature is medically robust enough to identify cancer mathematically using three fundamentally different algorithm classes (Linear, Decision Trees, and Gradient Boosting).

- **Precision (PPV)**: If the model flagged a patient as Cancer, what is the % chance they actually had cancer?
- **Sensitivity (Recall/TPR)**: Out of all the True ctDNA+ samples, what % did the model successfully catch?
- **Specificity (TNR)**: Out of all the Healthy Normal / Negative samples, what % did the model successfully clear as Healthy? **(Evaluated using dynamic optimized thresholds)**
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
metric_records = []
if mres is not None:
    for prefix, nice_name in [("lr", "Logistic Regression"), ("rf", "Random Forest"), ("xgb", "XGBoost")]:
        report = mres.get(f"{prefix}_classification_report", {})
        if report and "1" in report:
            metric_records.append({
                "Model": nice_name,
                "Metric": "Precision (PPV)",
                "Score": report["1"]["precision"]
            })
            metric_records.append({
                "Model": nice_name,
                "Metric": "Sensitivity (Recall)",
                "Score": report["1"]["recall"]
            })
            metric_records.append({
                "Model": nice_name,
                "Metric": "F1-Score",
                "Score": report["1"]["f1-score"]
            })
            if "0" in report:
                metric_records.append({
                    "Model": nice_name,
                    "Metric": "Specificity (TNR)",
                    "Score": report["0"]["recall"]  # Recall of class 0 is specificity
                })
            metric_records.append({
                "Model": nice_name,
                "Metric": "Accuracy",
                "Score": report.get("accuracy", 0)
            })

if metric_records:
    perf_df = pd.DataFrame(metric_records)
    fig_perf = px.bar(
        perf_df, x="Metric", y="Score", color="Model", barmode="group",
        color_discrete_sequence=THEME["models"]
    )
    _ = fig_perf.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1]),
        title="Cross-Validated Performance Metrics"
    )
    show_plotly(fig_perf, filename="performance_metrics")
else:
    Markdown("> Detailed performance metrics not available in JSON output.")
```

### ROC Curves

```{python}
#| echo: false
y_score_dict = {}
if lr_preds is not None: y_score_dict["Logistic Regression"] = lr_preds
if rf_preds is not None: y_score_dict["Random Forest"] = rf_preds
if xgb_preds is not None: y_score_dict["XGBoost"] = xgb_preds

if y_shap is not None and len(y_score_dict) > 0:
    y_true_dict = {name: y_shap for name in y_score_dict.keys()}
    fig_roc = plot_roc_curves(y_true_dict, y_score_dict, title="Multi-Model ROC")
    _ = fig_roc.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, 
        height=600
    )
        # Color scheme adjustment
    colors = THEME["models"]
    for i, trace in enumerate(fig_roc.data):
        if trace.name != "Random":
            trace.line.color = colors[i % len(colors)]
    show_plotly(fig_roc, filename="roc_curve")
else:
    Markdown("> ROC data not available.")
```

### Probability Densities

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## 🔬 Clinical Interpretation: Probability Densities (Click to Expand)
This tracks the raw internal structural logic of the Random Forest Model using Kernel Density Estimation (KDE). 

If the model is confused, the **Positive** and **Negative** density curves will aggressively overlap in the middle (near 0.5)—meaning the physical biology of Healthy cfDNA is indistinguishable from this patient's ctDNA fragments. 
A clinically perfect diagnostic biomarker will natively violently pull all Negative samples towards probability `0.0` and all tumor DNA strictly towards probability `1.0`.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if rf_preds_all is not None and kde_labels is not None:
    from plotly.figure_factory import create_distplot
    
    probs = rf_preds_all
    hist_data = []
    group_labels = []
    colors = []
    
    for lbl in LABEL_ORDER:
        mask = kde_labels == lbl
        d = probs[mask]
        if len(d) > 2:
            hist_data.append(d)
            group_labels.append(lbl)
            colors.append(THEME["labels"].get(lbl, "gray"))
            
    if len(hist_data) > 0:
        fig_kde = create_distplot(hist_data, group_labels, colors=colors,
                                  show_hist=False, show_rug=False)
        _ = fig_kde.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            title="Random Forest Predicted Probability Density",
            xaxis_title="Predicted Probability (ctDNA+)",
            yaxis_title="Density",
            height=600
        )
        show_plotly(fig_kde, filename="kde_distribution")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Not enough samples per class for density plotting."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Prediction density data not available."))
```

### Interactive Confusion Matrices

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## 🧩 Methodological Context: 4-Tier Cutoff Validation (Click to Expand)
Shows exactly how mathematical models partition the True and False subsets physically counting every sample. 

The models categorizing this matrix combine all **4 labeling tiers** naturally (Negative = `Healthy/Possible-`, Positive = `True/Possible+`). To maximize specificity, we do not naively assume 0.5 is the correct boundary. Instead, the models are instantly strictly evaluated exactly at their highest precision optimal clinical threshold mathematically possible using Youden's J statistic.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None:
    # Build 1x3 subplots HEATMAP
    models = [("lr", "Logistic Regression"), ("rf", "Random Forest"), ("xgb", "XGBoost")]
    titles = []
    cms = []
    
    for pfx, name in models:
        cm = mres.get(f"{pfx}_confusion_matrix")
        if cm:
            opt = mres.get(f"{pfx}_optimal_threshold")
            title_text = f"{name}<br><sub>(Opt Cutoff: {opt:.3f})</sub>" if opt is not None else name
            titles.append(title_text)
            cms.append(np.array(cm))
            
    if cms:
        import io
        from contextlib import redirect_stdout
        
        with redirect_stdout(io.StringIO()):
            fig_cm = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=len(cms), subplot_titles=titles)
            labels = ["Negative (0)", "Positive (1)"]
            
            for i, cm in enumerate(cms):
                # Annotations matrix
                z_text = [[str(y) for y in x] for x in cm]

                trace = go.Heatmap(
                    z=cm, x=labels, y=labels,
                    text=z_text, texttemplate="%{text}",
                    colorscale="Blues" if i==0 else ("Reds" if i==1 else "Oranges"),
                    showscale=False
                )
                _ = fig_cm.add_trace(trace, row=1, col=i+1)
                
            _ = fig_cm.update_layout(
                **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
                title="Cross-Validated Confusion Matrices (Absolute Counts)",
                height=400
            )
            for i in range(len(cms)):
                _ = fig_cm.update_xaxes(title_text="Predicted Label", row=1, col=i+1)
                _ = fig_cm.update_yaxes(title_text="True Label", row=1, col=i+1, autorange="reversed")
            
        show_plotly(fig_cm)
    else:
        Markdown("> Confusion matrices not found in JSON output.")
```

### Cancer Type Yield

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## 🧬 Generalization Analysis: Tissue Isolation (Click to Expand)
Certain fragment sizes shed completely uniquely across distinct metastatic origins safely. 
This strictly scopes the top 10 most frequent cancer types in your cohort, graphing independent Sensitivity metrics across Random Forest isolated validation subsets. 
Use this to identify whether this specific biomarker generalized broadly across all solid tumors universally, or if it acts specifically as a targeted marker solely for specific cancer tissues (e.g. solely Breast or Colorectal).
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None and "cancer_type_stats" in mres:
    ct_stats = mres["cancer_type_stats"]
    if ct_stats:
        ct_df = pd.DataFrame(ct_stats)
        if "rf_sensitivity" in ct_df.columns:
            melt_cols = ["cancer_type", "n_positives", "n_samples"]
            val_vars = ["rf_sensitivity"]
            if "xgb_sensitivity" in ct_df.columns:
                val_vars.append("xgb_sensitivity")
                
            long_df = pd.melt(ct_df, id_vars=melt_cols, value_vars=val_vars, var_name="Model", value_name="Sensitivity")
            long_df["Model"] = long_df["Model"].map({"rf_sensitivity": "Random Forest", "xgb_sensitivity": "XGBoost"})
            long_df = long_df.dropna(subset=["Sensitivity"])
            
            if not long_df.empty:
                # Sort primarily by RF
                sort_order = ct_df.sort_values(by="rf_sensitivity", ascending=True)["cancer_type"].tolist()
                fig_ct = px.bar(
                    long_df, x="Sensitivity", y="cancer_type", color="Model", orientation="h",
                    barmode="group",
                    hover_data=["n_positives", "n_samples"],
                    category_orders={"cancer_type": sort_order},
                    color_discrete_map=THEME["model_map"],
                    title="Sensitivity by Cancer Subgroup"
                )
                _ = fig_ct.update_layout(
                    **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
                    xaxis_title="Sensitivity (Recall)",
                    yaxis_title="",
                    height=600,
                    legend_title=""
                )
                show_plotly(fig_ct, filename="sensitivity_by_cancer")
            else:
                display(Markdown("> No valid sensitivity calculable per subgroup."))
        else:
             display(Markdown("> Subgroup missing sensitivity measurements."))
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Cancer Yield array is empty."))
```



### Assay Yield

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## 🧪 Methodological Context: Assay Variations (Click to Expand)
This metric natively stratifies the isolated Random Forest Sensitivity solely over differing targeted sequencing extraction assays (e.g. MSK-ACCESS v1 vs v2).
Use this graph to strictly eliminate technical coverage bias.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None and "assay_stats" in mres:
    a_stats = mres["assay_stats"]
    if a_stats:
        a_df = pd.DataFrame(a_stats)
        if "rf_sensitivity" in a_df.columns:
            melt_cols = ["assay", "n_positives", "n_samples"]
            val_vars = ["rf_sensitivity"]
            if "xgb_sensitivity" in a_df.columns:
                val_vars.append("xgb_sensitivity")
                
            long_df = pd.melt(a_df, id_vars=melt_cols, value_vars=val_vars, var_name="Model", value_name="Sensitivity")
            long_df["Model"] = long_df["Model"].map({"rf_sensitivity": "Random Forest", "xgb_sensitivity": "XGBoost"})
            long_df = long_df.dropna(subset=["Sensitivity"])
            
            if not long_df.empty:
                # Sort primarily by RF
                sort_order = a_df.sort_values(by="rf_sensitivity", ascending=True)["assay"].tolist()
                fig_a = px.bar(
                    long_df, x="Sensitivity", y="assay", color="Model", orientation="h",
                    barmode="group",
                    hover_data=["n_positives", "n_samples"],
                    category_orders={"assay": sort_order},
                    color_discrete_map=THEME["model_map"],
                    title="Sensitivity by Assay"
                )
                _ = fig_a.update_layout(
                    **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
                    xaxis_title="Sensitivity (Recall)",
                    yaxis_title="",
                    height=450,
                    legend_title=""
                )
                show_plotly(fig_a)
            else:
                display(Markdown("> No valid sensitivity calculable per assay."))
        else:
             display(Markdown("> Assay stats missing sensitivity measurements."))
    else:
        display(Markdown("> No assay subgroup statistics present."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Assay metrics not evaluated by engine."))
```

### Classification Report

```{python}
#| echo: false
import itables

if mres is not None:
    rep_dfs = []
    for prefix, name in [("lr", "Logistic Regression"), ("rf", "Random Forest"), ("xgb", "XGBoost")]:
        d = mres.get(f"{prefix}_classification_report")
        if d:
            t = pd.DataFrame(d).transpose()
            t["Model"] = name
            t.index.name = "Class/Metric"
            t = t.reset_index()
            rep_dfs.append(t)
            
    if rep_dfs:
        combined = pd.concat(rep_dfs, ignore_index=True)
        # Reorder columns
        cols = ["Model", "Class/Metric", "precision", "recall", "f1-score", "support"]
        combined = combined[[c for c in cols if c in combined.columns]]
        combined.replace({"0": "Healthy Normal", "1": "True ctDNA+"}, inplace=True)
        itables.show(combined.round(3), classes="display nowrap", maxBytes=0)
    else:
        Markdown("> Detailed classification reports unavailable.")
```

### Top Predictive Features

```{python}
#| echo: false
feat_list = "\n".join([f"- `{f}`" for f in top_feats])
Markdown(f"Features mapped across Models:\n\n{feat_list}")
```


# Feature Explainer UI (SHAP) {orientation="rows" scrolling="false"}

::: {.callout-info collapse="true" icon="false"}
## 🤖 Deep Learning Explainability: SHAP Interactions
**Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP)** physically unboxes algorithmic black-box models. These plots prove exactly *why* the mathematical architecture labeled a specific genomic sequence as 'Cancer'. They reveal raw underlying molecular biological interactions happening within human fragment distributions. (Powered by RF TreeExplainer implementations).
:::

## Row {.tabset}


```{python}
#| echo: false
# SHAP Helper functions
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

def compute_shap_and_plot_beeswarm(model, X_sh, shap_df_samp, model_title="", n_display=10):
    if model is None or shap_df_samp is None:
        return None, None, None, None
    try:    
        exp = shap.TreeExplainer(model)
        sh_vals = exp.shap_values(X_sh, check_additivity=False)

        # Robust binary output handling: may be list, 3D array, or 2D array
        if isinstance(sh_vals, list):
            sv_values = sh_vals[1]
        elif getattr(sh_vals, "ndim", 0) == 3:
            sv_values = sh_vals[:, :, 1]
        else:
            sv_values = sh_vals

        # Safe expected_value extraction for binary models
        base_val = exp.expected_value
        if isinstance(base_val, (list, np.ndarray)):
            base_val = base_val[1] if len(base_val) > 1 else base_val[0]

        sv = shap.Explanation(
            values=sv_values,
            base_values=base_val,
            data=X_sh.values,
            feature_names=X_sh.columns.tolist(),
        )
        
        feature_names = X_sh.columns.tolist()
        vals = np.abs(sv.values).mean(axis=0)
        # Only display top N features by mean |SHAP|, even though all were computed
        top_idx = np.argsort(vals)[-n_display:]
        top_features = [feature_names[i] for i in top_idx]
        
        sample_labels = shap_df_samp["label"].values
        s_ids = shap_df_samp["sample_id"].values if "sample_id" in shap_df_samp.columns else shap_df_samp.index.values
        
        records = []
        for rank, i in enumerate(top_idx):
            feature = feature_names[i]
            for j in range(len(sv.values)):
                records.append({
                    'Feature': feature,
                    'Feature_Idx': rank,
                    'SHAP Impact': float(sv.values[j, i]),
                    'Biomarker Value': float(sv.data[j, i]) if sv.data is not None else 0.0,
                    'True Label': sample_labels[j],
                    'Sample ID': s_ids[j]
                })
        
        df_sv = pd.DataFrame(records)
        df_sv['Feature Value'] = df_sv.groupby('Feature')['Biomarker Value'].transform(lambda x: (x - x.min()) / (x.max() - x.min() + 1e-8))
        df_sv['Y'] = df_sv['Feature_Idx'] + np.random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25, size=len(df_sv))
        
        fig = px.scatter(df_sv, x='SHAP Impact', y='Y', color='Feature Value', 
                       hover_data=['Sample ID', 'True Label', 'Feature', 'Biomarker Value'],
                       color_continuous_scale=[[0, '#1E88E5'], [0.5, '#9C27B0'], [1, '#E53935']])
        fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=4, opacity=0.8, line=dict(width=0)))
        fig.update_layout(
            title=f"<b>{model_title}</b>: SHAP Importance", title_x=0.5,
            plot_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], paper_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], font_color=THEME['text'], height=500,
            xaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'], zerolinecolor=THEME['axis'], zerolinewidth=2),
            yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, title='', tickmode='array', tickvals=list(range(len(top_features))), ticktext=top_features),
            coloraxis_colorbar=dict(title=""), margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=20)
        )
        show_plotly(fig)
        return sv, top_idx, sample_labels, s_ids
    except Exception as e:
        display(Markdown(f"> Error computing SHAP for {model_title}: {e}"))
        return None, None, None, None

def plot_shap_dependence(sv, top_idx, feature_names, sample_labels, s_ids, model_title=""):
    if sv is None: return
    feat1 = feature_names[top_idx[-1]]
    top_2_idx = top_idx[-2] if len(top_idx) > 1 else top_idx[-1]
    feat2 = feature_names[top_2_idx]
    
    df_dep = pd.DataFrame({
        feat1: sv.data[:, top_idx[-1]], 'SHAP Impact': sv.values[:, top_idx[-1]],
        feat2: sv.data[:, top_2_idx], 'True Label': sample_labels, 'Sample ID': s_ids
    })
    fig = px.scatter(df_dep, x=feat1, y='SHAP Impact', color=feat2, hover_data=['Sample ID', 'True Label'],
                     color_continuous_scale=[[0, '#1E88E5'], [0.5, '#9C27B0'], [1, '#E53935']])
    fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=5, opacity=0.8, line=dict(width=0)))
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"<b>{model_title}</b><br>Interaction: {feat1} vs {feat2}", title_x=0.5,
        plot_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], paper_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], font_color=THEME['text'], height=450,
        xaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'], zerolinecolor=THEME['axis'], zerolinewidth=2),
        yaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'], zerolinecolor=THEME['axis'], zerolinewidth=2),
        margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=50, b=20)
    )
    show_plotly(fig)

def plot_shap_waterfall(sv, probs, feature_names, sample_labels, model_title=""):
    if sv is None: return
    df_tracker = pd.DataFrame({'True Label': sample_labels, 'Probability': probs})
    
    tp_mask = df_tracker['True Label'] == "True ctDNA+"
    best_tp_idx = df_tracker[tp_mask]['Probability'].idxmax() if tp_mask.sum() > 0 else 0
    
    fp_mask = df_tracker['True Label'] == "Healthy Normal"
    worst_fp_idx = df_tracker[fp_mask]['Probability'].idxmax() if fp_mask.sum() > 0 else 0
    
    base_val = sv.base_values[0] if isinstance(sv.base_values, np.ndarray) and sv.base_values.size>0 else (sv.base_values if np.isscalar(sv.base_values) else 0)
    
    fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=(
        f"<b>True Positive</b> (Prob: {probs[best_tp_idx]:.2f})",
        f"<b>False Positive</b> (Prob: {probs[worst_fp_idx]:.2f})"
    ))
    
    for row_idx, s_idx in enumerate([best_tp_idx, worst_fp_idx]):
        shap_vals = sv.values[s_idx]
        order = np.argsort(np.abs(shap_vals))[-10:] 
        x = ["Baseline"] + [feature_names[i] for i in order] + ["Final"]
        y = [base_val] + [shap_vals[i] for i in order] + [base_val + np.sum(shap_vals)]
        
        hover_text = [f"Baseline: {base_val:.2f}"]
        for i in order:
            feat_val = sv.data[s_idx, i] if sv.data is not None else 0
            hover_text.append(f"Val: {feat_val:.3f}<br>Push: {shap_vals[i]:+.3f}")
        hover_text.append(f"Final: {y[-1]:.2f}")
        
        measure = ["absolute"] + ["relative"] * len(order) + ["total"]
        waterfall_trace = go.Waterfall(
            orientation="v", measure=measure, x=x, y=y, textposition="none", hovertext=hover_text, hoverinfo="x+text",
            decreasing={"marker":{"color":"#1E88E5"}}, increasing={"marker":{"color":"#E53935"}}, totals={"marker":{"color":"#9C27B0"}}
        )
        fig.add_trace(waterfall_trace, row=1, col=row_idx+1)
        
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"<b>{model_title}</b>: Top Confidence Traces", title_x=0.5,
        plot_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], paper_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], font_color=THEME['text'], height=450, showlegend=False, margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=60, b=20)
    )
    fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=False, tickangle=-45)
    fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'])
    show_plotly(fig)
```

### Biomarker Thresholds (Beeswarm)

```{python}
#| echo: false
rf_sv, rf_top, rf_sl, rf_sid = compute_shap_and_plot_beeswarm(rf_model, X_shap, shap_df_sample, "Random Forest", n_display=_shap_display_n) if rf_model is not None else (None, None, None, None)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import gc; gc.collect()  # Reclaim RF SHAP memory before XGB
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
xgb_sv, xgb_top, xgb_sl, xgb_sid = compute_shap_and_plot_beeswarm(xgb_model, X_shap, shap_df_sample, "XGBoost", n_display=_shap_display_n) if xgb_model is not None else (None, None, None, None)
```

### Top Feature Dependence

```{python}
#| echo: false
plot_shap_dependence(rf_sv, rf_top, X_shap.columns.tolist(), rf_sl, rf_sid, "Random Forest") if rf_sv is not None else None
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
plot_shap_dependence(xgb_sv, xgb_top, X_shap.columns.tolist(), xgb_sl, xgb_sid, "XGBoost") if xgb_sv is not None else None
```

### Error Post-Mortem (Waterfalls)

```{python}
#| echo: false
plot_shap_waterfall(rf_sv, rf_preds, X_shap.columns.tolist(), rf_sl, "Random Forest") if rf_sv is not None else None
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
plot_shap_waterfall(xgb_sv, xgb_preds, X_shap.columns.tolist(), xgb_sl, "XGBoost") if xgb_sv is not None else None
```


# Global Biomarker Yield {orientation="columns" scrolling="true"}

## Column {width="60%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Metric Significance (Volcano Effect Landscape)"
if eval_df is not None and "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in eval_df.columns:
    eval_volcano = eval_df.copy()
    if "kw_pvalue" in eval_volcano.columns:
        eval_volcano["-log10(p)"] = -np.log10(eval_volcano["kw_pvalue"].replace(0, 1e-300))
        eval_volcano["is_top"] = eval_volcano["feature_column"] == top_col

        eval_volcano["hover_text"] = eval_volcano.apply(
            lambda row: (f"<b>{row['feature_column']}</b><br>"
                         f"Cohen's D: {row['cohens_d_true_vs_healthy']:.2f}<br>"
                         f"K-W p-value: {row['kw_pvalue']:.3e}"), axis=1
        )

        fig_vol = px.scatter(
            eval_volcano, x="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", y="-log10(p)",
            hover_name="hover_text", color="is_top",
            color_discrete_map={True: THEME["labels"]["True ctDNA+"], False: "#515865"}
        )
        # Reference lines: p=0.01 significance + Cohen's D=0.8 large effect
        _ = fig_vol.add_hline(y=-np.log10(0.01), line_dash="dash", line_color="#8DDA77", opacity=0.5)
        _ = fig_vol.add_vline(x=0.8, line_dash="dash", line_color="#8DDA77", opacity=0.5)

        _ = fig_vol.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            showlegend=False,
            margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=30, b=10),
            xaxis_title="Cohen's D Effect Size (Larger is better)",
            yaxis_title="-Log10(Kruskal-Wallis P-Value)"
        )
        show_plotly(fig_vol)
    else:
        Markdown("> Kruskal-Wallis p-values not available in evaluation stats.")
else:
    Markdown("> Evaluation statistics not found.")
```

## Column {width="40%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Top 20 Features by Predictive Separation"
if eval_df is not None and "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in eval_df.columns:
    top_20_df = eval_df.sort_values(by="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", ascending=False).head(20)
    top_20_df = top_20_df.iloc[::-1]

    fig_bar = px.bar(
        top_20_df, x="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", y="feature_column",
        orientation='h',
        color="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy",
        color_continuous_scale="Purpor"
    )
    _ = fig_bar.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Cohen's D", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_bar)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Extracted Feature Statistical Ledger (Click to Expand)
```{python}
#| echo: false
if eval_df is not None:
    display_cols = ["feature_column", "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", "kw_pvalue",
                    "mwu_True_ctDNA_pos_vs_Healthy_Normal_effect_size", "spearman_vaf_r"]
    avail = [c for c in display_cols if c in eval_df.columns]
    eval_sorted = eval_df.sort_values(by="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", ascending=False)
    itables.show(eval_sorted[avail].round(4), classes="display nowrap dataTable", maxBytes=0)
```
:::

# Feature #1 Diagnostic Validation

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
d_val = best['cohens_d_true_vs_healthy'].iloc[0] if eval_df is not None and len(best) > 0 else 0
```

## Row {height="15%"}
```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Highest Statistically Separating Feature"
dict(value=top_col, color="primary")
```

## Row {height="45%"}

### Column
```{python}
#| title: "Metric Stratification Model (Violin)"
if top_col:
    fig_v = px.violin(
        df, x="label", y=top_col, color="label",
        box=True, points="outliers",
        category_orders={"label": LABEL_ORDER},
        color_discrete_map=THEME["labels"]
    )
    _ = fig_v.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_v)
```

### Column
```{python}
#| title: "Density Distribution Array"
if top_col:
    fig_dn = go.Figure()
    for label in LABEL_ORDER:
        if label not in df["label"].values:
            continue
        subset = df[df["label"] == label][top_col].dropna()
        if len(subset) > 5:
            _ = fig_dn.add_trace(go.Violin(
                y=subset, name=label, side="positive",
                line_color=THEME["labels"].get(label, "gray"),
                meanline_visible=True, width=1.2, opacity=0.8
            ))
    _ = fig_dn.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        violinmode="overlay",
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_dn)
```

## Row {height="40%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Independence from Tumor Burden Confounding (VAF Scatter)"
if top_col and "max_vaf" in df.columns:
    cancer = df[df["max_vaf"] > 0]
    if len(cancer) > 0:
        fig_vaf = px.scatter(
            cancer, x="max_vaf", y=top_col, color="label",
            color_discrete_map=THEME["labels"], opacity=0.6,
            trendline="lowess", log_x=True
        )
        _ = fig_vaf.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10)
        )
        show_plotly(fig_vaf)
```

# Cohort Summary Log

## Row
### Column {width="30%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Analyzed Cohort Size"
dict(value=f"{len(df):,}", color="primary", icon="people")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Molecular Sub-Metrics Gathered"
dict(value=f"{len(numeric_cols)}", color="info", icon="activity")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "CTDNA-Positive Determinations"
positive = sum(df["label"].isin(["True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+"]))
dict(value=f"{positive:,}", color="warning", icon="heart-pulse")
```

### Column {width="70%"}
```{python}
#| title: "Cancer Type Breakdown Directory"
if "CANCER_TYPE" in df.columns:
    c_counts = df["CANCER_TYPE"].value_counts().nlargest(15)

    fig_dc = px.bar(
        x=c_counts.values, y=c_counts.index, orientation='h',
        color=c_counts.values, color_continuous_scale="Teal"
    )
    _ = fig_dc.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Patient Samples", yaxis_title="",
        yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed")
    )
    show_plotly(fig_dc)
```

## Row
```{python}
#| title: "Multi-dimensional Catalog Matrix"
itables.show(df.head(500), classes="display nowrap", scrollX=True, scrollY="800px")
```
